title: Present continuous: I'm doing
explanation:
Present continuous

Use am / is / are + verb-ing for actions happening now or around now.

  • I am working.
  • She is reading.
  • They are watching TV.

Spelling: sit → sitting, run → running, make → making.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I [*am studying|study|studies] for my exam right now.
She [*is cooking|cook|cooks] dinner at the moment.
He [*isn't sleeping|don't sleep|doesn't sleeping] — he's watching TV.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Look! It ____. Take an umbrella! | *is raining | rains | rain
The children ____ in the garden. | *are playing | play | plays
I can't talk — I ____ a shower. | *am having | have | has
She ____ her homework at the moment. | *is doing | does | do

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: What [*are you doing|do you do|you are doing]?
questions.feedback: Question about now: What are you doing?

questions.text: I [*am not working|don't working|not work] this week.
